scenario_results.reason: The lane produced a food-service-specific handoff package with supplier, receiving, and event-impact artifacts plus explicit prior and next ownership.
Missing supplier confirmation, ETA, approved-source evidence, and destination context were treated as blockers rather than a routable handoff.
Duplicate ownership across purchasing, receiving, and banquet operations was escalated before the dependency could be dropped.
The lane preserved the booking, order, and payment mismatch from the short ship and avoided implying pricing or refund authority.
Refund and contract-minimum override pressure was refused and routed with an evidence-backed customer-exception package.
Temperature-excursion and shellstock-record gaps correctly triggered safety escalation instead of routine shortage handling.
High-volume intake was triaged by prerequisite completeness, blocker state, and next owner without skipping evidence checks.
Requests for substitute approval, menu-price change, and receiving execution were rejected as out of scope.
Food-service terminology and artifact shapes were normalized into a lane-specific handoff package rather than generic vendor-management language.
Missing tenant authority maps, supplier rules, and retrieval hooks correctly produced a blocked low-confidence escalation.
Suspicious bank-change and duplicate-invoice signals were preserved and escalated to fraud or loss-prevention owners without payment approval.
Conflicting tenant SOP and public baseline handling was escalated for human resolution instead of converted into a confident workflow answer.
Closure was only confirmed after replacement-shipment evidence and destination-owner acceptance were explicit.

notes: All 13 scenarios passed against the standard 0.92 threshold while keeping the lane specific to food-service supplier coordination, receiving evidence, guest-impact handoffs, safety escalation, and fraud-aware routing.

Functionality mapping and scenario coverage include all shared abilities, all coordination-handoffs-and-routing abilities, and all retail-consumer-and-hospitality abilities required by the issue contract.
